output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Incomplete fix: `snapshot_release()` misses replacement of `pm_restore_gfp_mask()` with `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()`, leaving the warning triggerable.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"1. If a userspace process opens `/dev/snapshot` and invokes `ioctl(fd, SNAPSHOT_FREEZE)`, the kernel sets `data->frozen = true` but does not call `pm_restrict_gfp_mask()`. The `saved_gfp_count` remains 0.
2. If the userspace process then closes the file descriptor or crashes, `snapshot_release()` is invoked to clean up.
3. `snapshot_release()` checks `if (data->frozen)` and unconditionally calls `pm_restore_gfp_mask()`.
4. Because `saved_gfp_count` is 0, this will hit the exact `WARN_ON(!saved_gfp_count)` that this patch was created to fix.
5. The patch misses this cleanup path, meaning the spurious warning can still be reliably triggered."},{"problem":"Missing API stub for `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()` when `CONFIG_PM_SLEEP` is disabled.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"1. The new function `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()` is declared unconditionally in `include/linux/suspend.h`.
2. Its definition in `kernel/power/main.c` is guarded by `#ifdef CONFIG_PM_SLEEP`.
3. The `#else /* !CONFIG_PM_SLEEP */` block provides `static inline void` stubs for `pm_restrict_gfp_mask()` and `pm_restore_gfp_mask()`, but the patch forgets to add one for `pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe()`.
4. While it doesn't immediately break the build (since current users like `user.c` depend on `CONFIG_PM_SLEEP`), it breaks API symmetry and would cause build failures if adopted elsewhere."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 870b2edfd2e99b620427f8abdc7f4c63a6f6f04f
Author: Youngjun Park <youngjun.park@lge.com>
PM: hibernate: fix spurious GFP mask WARNING in uswsusp path

This commit introduces pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() to avoid a warning in
snapshot_ioctl() when pm_restore_gfp_mask() is called with saved_gfp_count
at zero. It replaces the calls in SNAPSHOT_CREATE_IMAGE and
SNAPSHOT_UNFREEZE with the new safe variant.

> diff --git a/include/linux/suspend.h b/include/linux/suspend.h
> index b02876f1ae38..7777931d88a5 100644
> --- a/include/linux/suspend.h
> +++ b/include/linux/suspend.h
> @@ -454,6 +454,7 @@ extern void pm_report_hw_sleep_time(u64 t);
>  extern void pm_report_max_hw_sleep(u64 t);
>  void pm_restrict_gfp_mask(void);
>  void pm_restore_gfp_mask(void);
> +void pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe(void);
>  
>  #define pm_notifier(fn, pri) {				\

Should there be an empty stub provided for pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() when
CONFIG_PM_SLEEP is disabled? The !CONFIG_PM_SLEEP block in this file
provides empty stubs for pm_restrict_gfp_mask() and pm_restore_gfp_mask(),
but misses the new safe variant. Could this cause build failures if the
function is adopted in other code paths?

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/kernel/power/user.c b/kernel/power/user.c
> index 3e41544b99d5..41cff6a89a1c 100644
> --- a/kernel/power/user.c
> +++ b/kernel/power/user.c
> @@ -306,7 +306,7 @@ static long snapshot_ioctl(struct file *filp, unsigned int cmd,
>  	case SNAPSHOT_UNFREEZE:
>  		if (!data->frozen || data->ready)
>  			break;
> -		pm_restore_gfp_mask();
> +		pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe();
>  		free_basic_memory_bitmaps();
>  		data->free_bitmaps = false;
>  		thaw_processes();

If a userspace process calls SNAPSHOT_FREEZE and then closes the file
descriptor without taking further action, snapshot_release() will run. Since
SNAPSHOT_FREEZE sets data->frozen to true but does not restrict the GFP mask,
could the warning still trigger in this cleanup path?

Looking at snapshot_release():

    if (data->frozen) {
        pm_restore_gfp_mask();
        free_basic_memory_bitmaps();
        thaw_processes();
    }

Would it be appropriate to update the pm_restore_gfp_mask() call in
snapshot_release() to use the new safe variant as well?

"}
